questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: At 273 K, 10 g of glucose, 10 g of urea and 10 g of sucrose are separately dissolved in 250 mL of water. If their osmotic pressures are P1, P2 and P3, respectively, then the correct order is :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Blood is isotonic to which of the following?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: At 27°C, the osmotic pressure of blood is 7.40 atm. For preparing an intravenous glucose solution with osmotic pressure equal to that of blood, the moles of glucose required in one litre of solution are :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Which one of the following is most suitable for use as a semipermeable membrane?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: In red blood cells, the sum of concentrations of all dissolved particles is nearly 0.30 M, and the cell membrane allows only certain particles to pass through it. If these cells are taken out of blood plasma and kept in pure water at 298 K, what will be the osmotic pressure (in atmosphere) inside the cells?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Study the following cases :

I : 2M CH3COOH solution in benzene at 27°C in which complete dimer formation takes place

II : 0.5 M KCl aq. solution at 27°C, which undergoes 100% ionisation

Which statement(s) is/are correct :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: At 300 K, equal volumes of a M/20 glucose solution and a M/20 sucrose solution are combined, with the temperature remaining unchanged. If the osmotic pressures of the glucose solution, the sucrose solution and the resulting mixture are π1, π2 and π3, respectively, then
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Using the principle of reverse osmosis, estimate the minimum pressure needed to remove salt from sea water having 2.5% (w/v) NaCl at 27°C.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Statement I : Except osmotic pressure, all other colligative properties depend on the nature of solvent.

Statement II : Colligative properties are intensive properties.
options: Both statements are CORRECT, and Statement II is the CORRECT explanation of Statement I.
Both statements are CORRECT, and Statement II is NOT the CORRECT explanation of Statement I.
Statement I is CORRECT, but Statement II is INCORRECT.
Statement I is INCORRECT, but Statement II is CORRECT.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: A solution made by dissolving 4.0 g of PVC in 1 L of dioxane shows an osmotic pressure of 0.006 atm at 300 K. The molecular mass of the polymer PVC is
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: A $5\%$ solution of a solute (X) has the same osmotic pressure as a $0.855\%$ solution of sucrose (molecular weight $=342$). What is the molecular mass of solute (X)?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The osmotic pressure of two aqueous urea solutions-I and II are 2.1 atm and 1.8 atm respectively at $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. The osmotic pressure (in atm) of a solution at $25^{\circ}$ prepared by mixing solution I and II in the volume ratio of $2: 1$ is
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
The freezing point of an aqueous urea solution is found to be $-0.515^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. Calculate the osmotic pressure (in atm ) of this solution at $37^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$.( In Nearest Integer)

(Given : $K_{f\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)}=1.86 \mathrm{~K} \mathrm{~kg} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ )
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The osmotic pressure of a urea solution at $10^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ is 500 mm. After dilution of the solution, its temperature is increased to $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ and the osmotic pressure becomes 105.3 mm. Find the extent of dilution.
questions.subject: Chemistry
